name: Bank Of Monticello
description_short: Community bank in Northeast Missouri since 1928, offering personal and business banking with branches in Monticello, Canton, and Kahoka. Combines traditional service with modern digital banking.
description_long:
Bank of Monticello was organized by local shareholders in 1928 to serve families and businesses in Monticello, Missouri and surrounding communities. Over nearly a century, the bank has maintained its commitment to the communities it serves while expanding its geographic footprint and service offerings. The bank now operates three branches serving Lewis, Clark, and surrounding counties, positioning itself as a regional community financial institution.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of personal and business banking services. Personal banking includes checking and savings accounts, credit and debit cards, loans, and safe deposit boxes. They provide online banking, mobile banking, and mobile check deposit for digital customers. The business division offers agricultural and commercial loans, business deposit accounts with capture technology, and business credit cards. All customers have 24/7 access to night deposit boxes and ATM machines.

Bank of Monticello distinguishes itself through its emphasis on combining "big bank services with small town personal attention." The bank provides modern banking technology and products while maintaining local decision-making and personal relationships. They specifically market themselves to customers in rural Northeast Missouri, with extended hours at multiple locations and specialized agricultural lending expertise.

The bank operates standard community bank hours with some Saturday availability. Their service model depends on branch visits or digital channels, with limited information about loan products, rates, or terms publicly available on their website. As a regional community bank, they likely serve primarily local customers rather than offering nationwide online lending. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Mobile banking and mobile check deposit
pros: Three branch locations across Lewis and Clark counties in Northeast Missouri
24/7 access to ATMs, night deposit boxes, online banking, and mobile banking
Specialized agricultural and commercial lending for business customers
Mobile check deposit capability for convenient fund deposits
Nearly 100 years of local community banking experience since 1928
Extended hours at Canton and Kahoka locations (drive-up until 5:00 PM weekdays)
Business deposit capture technology for streamlined accounting
cons: Limited Saturday hours—Monticello location only open last Saturday of month
Website lacks transparent information on loan rates, terms, or account fees
Geographic service area limited to Northeast Missouri; not a nationwide online bank
No mention of competitive products like high-yield savings or money market accounts
Email communication explicitly stated as unsecured—customers must use online banking for sensitive financial information
best_for: Farmers and agricultural businesses needing specialized lending in Northeast Missouri
Local business owners in Lewis, Clark, and surrounding counties requiring personal banking relationships
Residents of Monticello, Canton, or Kahoka seeking community bank services with modern digital options
Customers prioritizing local decision-making and face-to-face service over online-only banking
diagnosis: Bank of Monticello is best for rural Northeast Missouri residents and agricultural/commercial businesses needing personalized, relationship-based banking in a community bank setting. The main caveat is that this is a regional community bank with limited geographic reach and minimal online information about products, rates, and fees—customers must visit branches or contact directly for specific details.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
